title: Delivery worker DNS check on every batch item
description:
LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:175

FINDING: is_private_host_resolved() is called once per delivery item per batch. That function calls tokio::net::lookup_host (a full DNS round-trip) for every inbox URI in every batch cycle. With 50 items per batch and a 5-second poll loop, that is up to 50 DNS lookups per 5 seconds from the delivery worker alone, even when all inbox domains are stable and already known-good. The SSRF defense at reqwest connect time (SsrfSafeResolver) already catches private IPs at the TCP layer; the pre-delivery DNS check is defense-in-depth, but its per-item cost is non-trivial.

IMPACT: Under load (many followers, rapid posting), the delivery worker spends a meaningful fraction of its time in DNS. Because delivery_queue is processed sequentially within a batch, each DNS round-trip adds latency before the next HTTP send can start. At 50 items/batch * ~10ms DNS = ~500ms added latency per batch cycle, which delays time-to-deliver under normal steady-state operation.
close_reason: Fixed: delivery worker no longer calls is_private_host_resolved pre-check; SsrfSafeResolver handles it
